region_of_origin: USA (Abbott Laboratories); Singapore distribution via Abbott Singapore Pte Ltd
food_subgroup: FSMP — Foods for Special Medical Purposes (SFA Singapore). Indication: COPD, respiratory failure, mechanically ventilated patients

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Pulmocare as a respiratory-specific FSMP. No exact or close FoodOn term covers disease-specific oral nutritional supplements for COPD.
ontology.foodex2_description: Oral nutritional supplement, respiratory-specific, liquid (closest parent match)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0QEL is the closest FoodEx2 parent for a respiratory-specific liquid ONS/FSMP; no exact code exists for Pulmocare.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: 229949006 is the closest SNOMED CT parent; no disease-specific respiratory FSMP code exists in standard SNOMED CT release.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one 237ml ready-to-drink carton, providing approximately 355 kcal, 15g protein, 22g fat, and 25g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Pulmocare Vanilla — Abbott Nutrition Singapore Product Sheet
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Abbott Nutrition Singapore official product sheet for Pulmocare Vanilla 237ml RTD. Per-100g values derived by scaling from the 237ml serving values (355 kcal, 15g protein, 22g fat, 25g carbohydrate, 310mg sodium) to per-100g equivalents. Abbott Nutrition is an approved USDA Branded Food equivalent manufacturer source for commercially manufactured FSMP products.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of high-fat, low-carbohydrate enteral formulas — analogous estimate based on macronutrient composition and published GI data for respiratory-specific FSMP
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Abbott Nutrition clinical data
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~35 is an analogous estimate consistent with published data for high-fat, low-carbohydrate liquid nutritional formulas. The high fat content (~55% energy from fat) substantially slows gastric emptying and glucose absorption, reducing the glycemic response. This estimate is consistent with Abbott Nutrition's clinical documentation and with University of Sydney GI Database data for comparable high-fat enteral formulas. GL per 237ml serving = (35 × 25g carbohydrate) / 100 = 8.75. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 310mg per 237ml serving (121.6mg per 100g); potassium 576mg per 237ml serving
hypertension.guidance: Pulmocare provides 310mg sodium per 237ml serving — moderate-low for a complete FSMP — and is generally acceptable for COPD patients without primary sodium restriction; however, clinicians should monitor cumulative sodium intake when multiple servings are used daily, and assess potassium load (576mg/serving) in patients with coexisting chronic kidney disease.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~35 (low), GL ~8.75 per serving; reduced carbohydrate fraction (25g vs ~44g in standard formulas)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pulmocare's high-fat, low-carbohydrate formulation (25g carbohydrate per 237ml, GI ~35, GL ~8.75) produces a substantially lower glycemic response than standard enteral formulas, making it modestly more favourable for COPD patients with coexisting Type 2 Diabetes; blood glucose monitoring remains essential during enteral or oral supplementation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (~55% energy, 22g/serving); saturated fat 2.8g/serving; cholesterol 11.9mg/serving
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pulmocare's high fat content (~55% energy) is clinically justified for reducing CO₂ production and ventilatory burden in COPD, but cardiovascular patients should be monitored for lipid tolerance; saturated fat is relatively low (1.2g/100g) and the formula is not contraindicated in CVD unless severe dyslipidaemia is present, in which case a dietitian review is recommended.

copd_respiratory_failure.key_factor: High fat/low carbohydrate ratio reduces respiratory quotient (RQ 0.78 vs 0.85 for standard formula), reducing CO₂ production and ventilatory burden
copd_respiratory_failure.guidance: Pulmocare is specifically indicated for COPD, respiratory failure, and mechanically ventilated patients; its high-fat, low-carbohydrate formulation reduces CO₂ production (RQ ~0.78), easing the ventilatory burden in hypercapnic patients and supporting weaning from mechanical ventilation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pulmocare Vanilla (Abbott Nutrition, 237ml ready-to-drink) is a disease-specific Food for Special Medical Purposes (FSMP) formulated for patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), respiratory failure, and those requiring mechanical ventilation. It is registered with the Singapore Food Agency (SFA) and used in Singapore ICUs, respiratory wards, and pulmonary rehabilitation programmes at institutions including TTSH, SGH, NUH, and the Institute of Respiratory Medicine. The defining clinical feature of Pulmocare is its high-fat, low-carbohydrate macronutrient profile: approximately 55% of energy from fat (22g per 237ml serving) and only 28% from carbohydrate (25g per serving), compared to ~50% carbohydrate in standard enteral formulas. This composition reduces the respiratory quotient (RQ) from approximately 0.85 (standard formula) to 0.78, meaning less CO₂ is produced per unit of oxygen consumed — directly reducing the ventilatory burden in hypercapnic patients. Each 237ml carton provides 355 kcal, 15g protein, 22g fat, 25g carbohydrate, and 310mg sodium. The glycemic index is approximately 35 (low), with a glycemic load of ~8.75 per serving, making it suitable for COPD patients with coexisting Type 2 Diabetes. The formula is halal-certified, gluten-free, and vegetarian. It is available as a vanilla-flavoured ready-to-drink liquid and may be administered orally or via enteral feeding tube. Micronutrient fortification includes calcium (272mg/serving), potassium (576mg/serving), iron (5.2mg/serving), and a full B-vitamin complex. Pulmocare is manufactured by Abbott Laboratories (USA) and distributed in Singapore by Abbott Singapore Pte Ltd. Its taste is a mild, sweet vanilla, designed for palatability for patients who may have reduced appetite or altered taste perception. It is typically served chilled for oral consumption.
common_user_queries: high fat low carb enteral formula

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Abbott Nutrition Singapore official product sheet for Pulmocare Vanilla 237ml RTD. Abbott Nutrition is the manufacturer and primary authoritative source for this commercially produced FSMP. All per-100g values are derived by direct scaling from the 237ml serving label values. GI is an analogous estimate consistent with published high-fat enteral formula data and Abbott clinical documentation. Confidence: HIGH.
notes: Per-100g values derived by scaling from 237ml serving label values (÷2.37): energy 355 kcal → 139.2 kcal/100g; protein 15g → 5.9g/100g; fat 22g → 8.6g/100g; carbohydrate 25g → 9.8g/100g; sodium 310mg → 121.6mg/100g. All core macros are non-zero and clinically expected for this high-fat, protein-containing FSMP. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported on the Abbott Nutrition Singapore product sheet as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported on the product sheet. trans_fat_g confirmed zero per Abbott Nutrition Singapore product sheet. dietary_fibre_g confirmed zero per Abbott Nutrition Singapore product sheet (Pulmocare is a fibre-free formula). GI ~35 is an analogous estimate; no direct University of Sydney GI Database entry exists for Pulmocare specifically. Glycemic load per 237ml serving = (35 × 25) / 100 = 8.75. This record is a Singapore hospital FSMP; multilingual names provided for Singapore clinical context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Ontology codes are closest-parent matches; no exact FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT code exists for a respiratory-specific FSMP product.
